steps.title: Read the sign of the slope and y-intercept
steps.content: The line of best fit rises from left to right, so the slope is positive. It crosses the y-axis just above zero, so the y-intercept is positive.

steps.title: Estimate y-intercept and slope
steps.content: Use two clear points on the line of best fit, such as $(2,4.2)$ and $(5,9)$, and run a quick linear regression: $x_1=[2,5]$, $y_1=[4.2,9]$, $y_1\sim mx_1+b$. Desmos gives $m\approx1.6$ and $b\approx1$, close to the choice $y=0.8+1.7x$.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• A) $y=0.8+1.7x$ — positive y-intercept, positive slope. Matches.
  • B) $y=0.8-1.7x$ — slope negative; the line rises, eliminate.
  • C) $y=-0.8+1.7x$ — y-intercept negative; line crosses above zero, eliminate.
  • D) $y=-0.8-1.7x$ — both wrong signs, eliminate.
That matches choice A — A is correct.
